displayText.simplePara: AMPEL360e Wide Tube-and-Wing Family (eWTW) — All variants

remarks.simplePara: Programme-controlled placeholder. infoCode 040A subject to BREX/SNS/DMRL freeze. Not for operational use.

levelledPara.title: General
levelledPara.para: This data module describes the Compression System of the AMPEL360e eWTW Environmental Control System (ECS), aligned to ATLAS node 020-010 and ATA 21-10. The compression system is the air supply source for the ECS, providing pressurized conditioned bleed air or electrically compressed air to the air conditioning packs.

levelledPara.title: Compression Architecture
levelledPara.para: The eWTW compression subsystem architecture supports the following air supply modes depending on the variant configuration:

listItem.para: Engine bleed air extraction from high-pressure compressor stages (conventional and SAF modes).
Electric compressor packs driven by the aircraft electrical power system (hybrid-electric modes).
Bleedless architecture option for advanced eWTW variants, eliminating engine bleed and relying entirely on electric compressors.

levelledPara.title: Key Components
levelledPara.para: Major components of the compression subsystem include:

listItem.para: Bleed air valves (HP and IP stage selection).
Pre-coolers and heat exchangers (engine bleed cooling).
Air conditioning packs (heat exchangers, turbines, compressors).
Pack bypass valves and mixing chambers.

levelledPara.title: eWTW Hybrid-Electric Interface
levelledPara.para: In hybrid-electric operating modes, the electrical system supplies power to the electric compressor packs. This creates a load coupling between the ECS and the power management system that must be managed by the aircraft energy management controller. The ECS compression demands are communicated to the power management system to enable dynamic load balancing.
